pages.text: Three years ago, a blazing object tore through the sky and slammed into the earth near civilization. The explosion leveled trees, shattered stone, and left a smoldering crater where fields once stood.$(br2)When the smoke cleared, something unusual was embedded in the ground around the impact site: strange metallic fragments, cooling from white-hot to deep cyan.

Scientists were baffled. The material was like nothing known on Earth — impossibly dense, yet lightweight. It resonated faintly with biological tissue, as if it were designed to interact with living organisms.$(br2)They called it $(bold)Nano Alloy$().

But the metal was just the vessel. Inside the asteroid, embedded in the alloy's crystalline structure, were microscopic machines — $(bold)nanobots$() — woven from the alloy itself. Advanced beyond our understanding. Sentient, perhaps, in their own way.$(br2)They were not products of Earth. They were not hostile, either. They were searching for something.

Governments scrambled to secure the crash site. Military cordons, research facilities, quarantine zones. Too late.$(br2)Before containment was established, the nanobots had already spread. The swarms scattered across the land, drawn by some strange attraction to biological life. They moved like insects, like water, like nothing nature had ever produced.$(br2)And they found targets.

One of those machines found $(italic)you$().$(br2)A single NanoCreature approached — a construct of shimmering metal no larger than your fist — studied you for a moment, and then $(bold)merged with your body$(). You blacked out.$(br2)When you woke up, you felt different. Stronger. Your muscles burned with unfamiliar power. Your thoughts moved faster. Your senses extended beyond normal human limits.

You are no longer entirely human.$(br2)But you are not enslaved. The nanobots did not take you over — they $(bold)elevated$() you. They offered a partnership written in the language of biology itself. They see potential in you. They want to see what you can become.$(br2)The question is: what will $(italic)you$() do with this power?
